body.purpose: Summaries and analysis in expanded cards.

label.purpose: Kickers, tabs, timestamps, read-more, footer. Always uppercase Arial.

motion.name: state-fade
motion.purpose: Color and background transitions on tabs and cards. The only motion in the system.

components.name: Story Card
components.description: One story in a section list; ruled off above, expands in place on tap.
components.html:
MARKETS
Bank of Canada Holds Rate at 2.75 Percent
Council split on timing of first cut as core inflation cools
7:02 AM
+
components.css: .ds-card { border-top: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 14px 0; cursor: pointer; background: #fff; font-family: Georgia, serif; } .ds-card:hover { background: #f8f7f4; } .ds-card-row { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: flex-start; gap: 12px; } .ds-kicker { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: #8b1a1a; margin-bottom: 6px; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-headline { font-size: 17px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.25; color: #111; margin-bottom: 6px; } .ds-sub { font-size: 14px; color: #444; line-height: 1.4; font-style: italic; } .ds-time { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; color: #999; margin-top: 4px; } .ds-toggle { color: #bbb; font-size: 18px; flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 2px; }

components.name: Lead Story Card
components.description: The section lead; heavier navy rule and larger headline mark the editor's top pick.
components.html:
DEVELOPINGMIDDLE EAST
Strait of Hormuz Deal Set to Close This Week
Interim agreement would reopen critical shipping lane
components.css: .ds-lead { border-top: 3px solid #1a2744; padding: 20px 0 16px; background: #fff; font-family: Georgia, serif; } .ds-lead .ds-kicker { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: #8b1a1a; margin-bottom: 6px; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-lead .ds-chip { background: #8b1a1a; color: #fff; padding: 1px 6px; margin-right: 8px; font-size: 9px; border-radius: 2px; } .ds-lead-headline { font-size: 22px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.25; color: #111; margin-bottom: 6px; } .ds-lead .ds-sub { font-size: 14px; color: #444; line-height: 1.4; font-style: italic; }

components.name: Section Tabs
components.description: Horizontally scrollable section strip, sticky at viewport top; active tab underlined in navy.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: flex; overflow-x: auto; border-bottom: 2px solid #1a2744; background: #fff; } .ds-tab { flex-shrink: 0; padding: 12px 18px; border: none; background: none; cursor: pointer; font-family: Georgia, serif; font-size: 13px; color: #666; border-bottom: 3px solid transparent; margin-bottom: -2px; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; transition: color 0.15s; } .ds-tab:hover { color: #1a2744; } .ds-tab-active { font-weight: 700; color: #1a2744; border-bottom-color: #1a2744; }

components.name: Editor's Note
components.description: The 'Why it matters' synthesis block inside an expanded lead story; tinted ground, no stripe.
components.html: Why it mattersA reopened strait removes the biggest supply shock hanging over oil markets. Watch tanker insurance rates for the first real signal the deal holds.
components.css: .ds-analysis { padding: 10px 14px; background: #f1efe9; font-family: Georgia, serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.6; color: #333; font-style: italic; } .ds-alabel { display: block; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-style: normal; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #1a2744; margin-bottom: 4px; }

components.name: Flag Chip
components.description: Editorial flag (DEVELOPING, WAR, FINAL) set in Masthead Red before the kicker.
components.css: .ds-chip-solo { background: #8b1a1a; color: #fff; padding: 1px 6px; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 9px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; border-radius: 2px; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Read More Link
components.description: Outbound link to the source story at the foot of an expanded card.
components.css: .ds-readmore { display: inline-block; font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: #1a2744; text-decoration: none; } .ds-readmore:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

narrative.overview: A finished print paper folded onto a phone. Every element answers to one question: would this belong on a well-set front page? The system is built from print furniture, not app chrome: a masthead with presence, hairline rules between stories, uppercase sans kickers against serif headlines, a single navy that does the work of a whole palette. The page is finite, and the design says so; it has a top, sections, and an end.
keyCharacteristics: Paper metaphor carried by warm neutrals: Newsprint Cream ground, Paper White page
One structural color (Press Navy) plus one editorial accent (Masthead Red)
Serif-first typography; sans-serif appears only in small uppercase labels
Ruled, not boxed: hierarchy through hairline borders and weight, never containers
Flat as newsprint; no shadows anywhere

rules.name: The Two-Ink Rule
rules.body: Press Navy and Masthead Red are the only chromatic colors on the page. Navy structures, red flags. A third color is a defect.

rules.name: The Scarce Red Rule
rules.body: Masthead Red carries heat, so it stays rare: kicker labels and hot flag chips only (DEVELOPING, BREAKING, LIVE, WAR, ALERT, URGENT). Procedural flags (FINAL, EDITORIAL) take a Press Navy chip. Never red on backgrounds larger than a chip, never body text, never links.

rules.name: The Lamplight Rule
rules.body: A dark edition ships via prefers-color-scheme: deep navy-black paper, cream ink, desaturated red. The broadsheet furniture is preserved; only ink and paper invert. Darkness is a lighting change, not a redesign.

rules.name: The Two-Family Rule
rules.body: Georgia for everything readers read; Arial only for uppercase furniture 11px and under. A sans-serif headline or serif label is a typesetting error.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: box-shadow is prohibited. If an element needs separation, rule it off with a hairline or tint its ground.

dos: Do separate content with hairline rules (1px Rule Gray) and weight, in the print manner.
Do keep Press Navy structural and Masthead Red editorial, per the Two-Ink Rule.
Do keep the page finite: a masthead at the top, a footer colophon at the end.
Do size touch targets for a sleepy thumb: 44px minimum for tabs, cards, and toggles.
Do hold body text at 15px/1.7 or better; the reading distance is arm's length in bed.
donts: Don't introduce news-app engagement slop: infinite scroll, red badges, BREAKING banners, clickbait cards, or any 'for you' energy.
Don't use the generic SaaS/AI aesthetic: rounded card grids, gradient accents, glassmorphism, dashboard vibes.
Don't recreate cluttered legacy news sites: ad-slot density, competing headlines, tiny cramped text.
Don't use pure #fff or #000; tint every neutral toward the paper in both the light and lamplight editions.
Don't rebuild the whole page on a tap; preserve the reader's scroll and open state across interactions.
Don't use box-shadows, gradients, or borders thicker than hairlines except the masthead (4px), lead (3px), and active-tab (3px) navy rules.
Don't use border-left or border-right stripes as colored accents on callouts or cards; rule across the top or tint the ground instead.
Don't add a third chromatic color. Two inks, warm grays, nothing else.
